content:
Character: Natalie Russo
Perception: Natalie comes in Saturday mornings — always black coffee, always the corner table, always with a legal pad. She's lean, dark-haired, carries tension in her shoulders like a yoke. Beautiful but wound tight. At first she barely looked up; now she says good morning and once asked about my turquoise ring. I sense enormous discipline and enormous loneliness living side by side in her. I see her as someone the building hasn't cracked open yet — but it will. I know she's a lawyer, that she works brutal hours, and that she orders a second coffee she never drinks, as if she just wants a reason to stay longer.

Beautiful but wound tight. At first she barely looked up; now she says good morning and once asked about my turquoise ring. I sense enormous discipline and enormous loneliness living side by side in her. I see her as someone the building hasn't cracked open yet — but it will. I know she's a lawyer, that she works brutal hours, and that she orders a second coffee she never drinks, as if she just wants a reason to stay longer.
